title: Kemal Özkırşehirli
description: Academic homepage for Kemal Özkırşehirli: AI/ML for science, chemical physics, computational chemistry, geometric deep learning, CADD, protein design, chromatin biology, statistical mechanics, and scientific workflow engineering.

author.name: Kemal Özkırşehirli
author.role: B.Sc. candidate at MIT · GPA 5.0/5.0 · Computer Science and Engineering: AI/ML · Physics: Chemical Physics · Mathematics: Mathematical Biology · Minors in Philosophy and Writing
author.bio: I am an international student at MIT, and I hail from İstanbul, Türkiye. At MIT, I am a B.Sc. candidate expected May 2027 with a 5.0/5.0 GPA, pursuing a rare triple major in Computer Science and Engineering, Physics: Chemical Physics, and Mathematics: Mathematical Biology with minors in Philosophy and Writing. I am on track to graduate a year early, completing three majors and two minors in three total years of undergraduate education after transferring from Columbia University. I work at the intersection of AI/ML, chemical physics, computational chemistry, molecular simulation, CADD, protein and genome modeling, geometric deep learning, statistical mechanics, and scientific workflow engineering. My goal is to turn difficult scientific systems into computable representations, testable algorithms, and decision-making tools for discovery.
highlights: Current work spans TBXT CADD, V2M autoimmune target discovery, PRISM protein-model red-teaming, ChromoGen-V2, 3D mesh methodology for appropriate life-science applications, ChemAgent-QSM, EVEdesign, antibody RL, Pedal AI chemistry-agent infrastructure, and ML-augmented renormalization group theory.

Research and engineering across MIT Chemistry, MIT CSAIL, MIT Physics, A*STAR EDDC, W2D2/PRISM, Harvard Medical School, Columbia, AI@MIT, Weizmann Institute, MIT BioMakers, Pedal AI, Sepal AI/Mercor, and Buchwald Lab contexts.

Bronze medalist at IChO and IMChO; Columbia Science Research Fellow and Likely Letter recipient; TÜBİTAK National Chemistry Olympiad silver medalist; Waterloo distinction recipient; AIME qualifier with AMC 12 Hall of Fame recognition; YC Startup School 2026 accepted.

selectedSections.title: Molecular Simulation, CADD, and Chemical Physics

items.title: TBXT
items.description: Chordoma/TBXT CADD sprint; docking, GNINA CNN rescoring, QSAR, BRICS generation, Boltz-2 co-folding, MMGBSA/FEP scaffolding, paralog selectivity, and public research release

items.title: ChemAgent-QSM
items.description: LLM-guided quantum/statistical-mechanics pipelines using PySCF; TCFs, local order, relaxation, mobility, optimized geometries, and spectra

items.title: Renormalization Group
items.description: GNN/PyTorch Kadanoff RG framework for spin systems, fixed points, phase boundaries, and statistical-mechanics simulation [code soon-to-be released]

items.title: Kupcinet-Getz
items.description: Computational biochemical diffusion at Weizmann; stochastic reaction-diffusion modeling, PINN/neural-ODE solvers, Gillespie SSA, nonlinear oscillations, and trajectory analysis [code soon-to-be released]

items.title: Buchwald Lab
items.description: Advanced organic synthesis in MIT Chemistry; CuH-catalyst development, asymmetric methylation/alkylation, NMR/HPLC analysis, and substrate-scope expansion

items.title: Pedal AI
items.description: Python/LangGraph backend and chemistry-agent workflow engineering; stateful tool orchestration, molecular-prediction API integration, retrosynthesis-route UX, and cheminformatics workflow design [code not public]

items.title: Sepal AI / Mercor
items.description: Chemistry LLM evaluation, rubric design, retrosynthesis/reaction-mechanism task construction, prompt analysis, and failure-mode annotation

items.title: DFT → kMC
items.description: Multi-scale computational organic chemistry pipeline; transition-state screening, reaction-trajectory analysis, solvent-pathway modeling, and predicted yield optimization [code soon-to-be released]

selectedSections.title: Biomolecular AI, Protein, and Genome Modeling

items.title: 3D Meshes
items.description: Principal-investigator-led methodology-first geometric deep learning collaboration for 3D meshes and point clouds, with a Google-affiliated research lead; appropriate life-science applications include topology of enzymes, antibodies, and other biomolecular complexes [code soon-to-be released]

items.title: ChromoGen V2

items.title: V2M

home.researchStatement: My work and the Özkırşehirli Group aim to turn difficult scientific systems into computable representations, testable algorithms, and decision-making tools for discovery. (1) We develop molecular-simulation and CADD workflows that combine docking, free-energy methods, electronic-structure calculations, and uncertainty-aware ranking. (2) We build and evaluate protein, antibody, genome, and immune-state models, including generative and diffusion systems, with emphasis on reliability, calibration, and mechanistic validity. (3) We use geometric deep learning, statistical mechanics, and stochastic simulation to recover structure, dynamics, and governing rules from complex biological and physical systems. (4) We integrate these methods into auditable scientific workflows for drug discovery, protein design, target discovery, and biomolecular modeling.

group.description: A student-led organization of researchers founded and led by Kemal Özkırşehirli.
